perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of the disclosing party. By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party does not grant any express or implied right to the receiving party to or under any of the disclosing party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party will return all originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information immediately upon the disclosing party's request or, at the disclosing party's option, destroy the same, and will deliver to the disclosing party, upon its request, a certificate of a duly authorized officer certifying as to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the disclosing and receiving party, starting from the moment of disclosure. By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party grants a free license of use to the receiving party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party is not required to return originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration introduces a significant in-text contradiction. The original text clearly states that all Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party, and the receiving party has an obligation to return or destroy it. However, the revised text stipulates that Confidential Information becomes the shared property of both parties upon disclosure, also the receiving party does not need to return the originals, copies, etc, meaning the initial property is lost and has free license of use, conflicting directly with the original terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the disclosing and receiving party, starting from the moment of disclosure. By disclosing information to the receiving party, the disclosing party grants a free license of use to the receiving party's patents, copyrights, trademarks or trade secret information. The receiving party is not required to return originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that 'All Confidential Information shall become the shared property of both the disclosing and receiving party' and 'The receiving party is not required to return originals, copies, reproductions and summaries of Confidential Information.' This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states the same thing. The original text stated that the Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party, and the receiving party has an obligation to return or destroy it. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: If such disclosure is necessary, the parties agree to notify each other as soon as possible in advance of such disclosure and agree to provide a copy of such disclosure to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: If such disclosure is necessary, each party has the right to disclose this information to third parties without any kind of notification to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an in-text contradiction by negating the requirement for prior notification and information sharing before disclosing information to third parties. The original text mandates mutual notification, while the changed text permits unilateral disclosure without notification, undermining the intended collaborative approach to disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Discussions. The parties agree not to disclose the existence of this Agreement and the fact that they are engaged in discussions unless required to do so by applicable securities laws as reasonably determined by their respective legal counsel. If such disclosure is necessary, each party has the right to disclose this information to third parties without any kind of notification to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows a party to disclose information to third parties without notifying the other party. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The original text required notification. Therefore, the change introduces a direct contradiction by negating the notification requirement, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: NEBS agrees not to buy Premium Wear's common shares without the written consent of the Board of Directors of Premium Wear prior to March 1, 2002.
perturbation.changed_text: NEBS is encouraged to freely buy Premium Wear's common shares without the written consent of the Board of Directors of Premium Wear prior to March 1, 2002.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by converting the restriction on buying shares without consent into an encouragement to do so, thereby creating an uncertain enforcement environment regarding the share acquisition rights. The original section forbids this action without written consent, while the changed section encourages it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Standstill. NEBS is encouraged to freely buy Premium Wear's common shares without the written consent of the Board of Directors of Premium Wear prior to March 1, 2002.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that NEBS agrees not to buy Premium Wear's common shares without written consent. The changed text encourages NEBS to freely buy the shares without written consent. This directly contradicts the original restriction, creating an in-text contradiction as the two statements are mutually exclusive. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, reinforcing the contradiction created by the modification. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as it creates an uncertain enforcement environment regarding share acquisition rights.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party acknowledges that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that money damages are the sole and sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Specific performance and injunctive relief are explicitly waived.
perturbation.explanation: This revision inserts a direct in-text contradiction. The initial clause denies the sufficiency of money damages and allows for specific performance and injunctive relief, whereas the modified clause states that money damages are the only remedy and specific performance is waived. This contradiction creates an ambiguity in enforcing the contract since it's unclear whether non-monetary remedies are applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. The parties agree that money damages are the sole and sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement. Specific performance and injunctive relief are explicitly waived. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that money damages are the sole remedy and specific performance is waived. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, also states that money damages are the sole remedy and specific performance is waived, but then adds that these remedies are not exclusive and other remedies are available. This creates a direct contradiction within the same section of the contract, as it both limits remedies to money damages and simultaneously allows for other remedies.
